jokes.text: Blah. Blee. Bleh. [Chuckles] Stupid animals.
jokes.setup: Homer is asked to discipline Bart for making faces
jokes.punchline: Homer responds by making his own childish faces and calling his family stupid animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - immature, oblivious to irony, completely missing the point of parenting

jokes.text: Ooh, the Springfield Men's Shelter is giving away 60 soiled mattresses.
jokes.setup: Homer excitedly reads the free column in newspaper
jokes.punchline: The 'deal' is disgusting used mattresses from a homeless shelter
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about poverty mixed with Homer's cheapskate nature

jokes.text: Why do you read that 'Free' column, Homer? They never have anything good.
jokes.setup: Marge questions Homer's obsession with free items
jokes.punchline: Sets up the immediate contradiction when Homer finds something exciting
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Homer's excitement about the trampoline. Solid structural comedy.

jokes.text: 'Tramampoline.' 'Trambapoline.'
jokes.setup: Homer tries to read 'trampoline' from newspaper
jokes.punchline: His mispronunciation reveals his excitement and poor reading skills
jokes.explanation: Iconic Simpsons moment. Homer's childlike mispronunciation became a cultural touchstone. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: He said what now? Please, don't bring home any more old crutches.
jokes.setup: Marge can't understand Homer's mispronunciation
jokes.punchline: Reveals Homer has a history of bringing home medical equipment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing Homer's pattern of terrible free acquisitions

jokes.text: Hey. That seltzer ain't free.
jokes.setup: Homer sprays himself with seltzer at Krusty's place
jokes.punchline: Krusty's casual concern about payment for seltzer water
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor about Krusty's cheapness

jokes.text: There once was a man named Enis-- I can just haul it away, free?
jokes.setup: Krusty starts telling a dirty limerick
jokes.punchline: Homer interrupts mid-setup, oblivious to the adult content
jokes.explanation: Good timing - Homer's obliviousness to adult humor is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Dad, this one gesture almost makes up for years of shaky fathering.
jokes.setup: Bart is thrilled about the trampoline
jokes.punchline: Casually acknowledges Homer's consistently poor parenting
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing - Bart's casual acceptance of bad parenting with gratitude for one good moment

jokes.text: I'm gonna have my wedding here.
jokes.setup: Family enjoying the trampoline
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old Lisa already planning her wedding venue
jokes.explanation: Child character humor - Lisa's premature adult planning

jokes.text: It'll double the value of our house... and it was free-- free!
jokes.setup: Homer defending the trampoline purchase
jokes.punchline: Absurd economics - free item doubling house value
jokes.explanation: Homer's flawed logic and obsession with 'free' things

jokes.text: It smells funny in there. No, it doesn't.
jokes.setup: Child complains about smell in Homer's carnival setup
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate defensive denial
jokes.explanation: Homer's knee-jerk defensiveness about his shoddy operation. Minor but in character.

jokes.text: Otto, are you okay? Yeah. Just pop my shoulder back in. [Cracks] Ooh. Thanks, buddy. Hey, I lost my turn.
jokes.setup: Otto gets injured on trampoline
jokes.punchline: Casual attitude toward serious injury and concern about missing his turn
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Otto's laid-back character response

jokes.text: Each leap brings us closer to God. Catch me, Lord. Catch me.
jokes.setup: People getting injured on trampoline
jokes.punchline: Ned finds spiritual meaning in dangerous bouncing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders - turning dangerous activity into religious experience

jokes.text: Kids, kids. Once you get hurt, move aside and let other people jump.
jokes.setup: Multiple people injured on trampoline
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution prioritizes fun over safety
jokes.explanation: Dark and brilliant - Homer's selfish priorities disguised as reasonable crowd control

jokes.text: Hey, Krusty. I'm bringing back the-- You just keep right on driving.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to return the trampoline to Krusty
jokes.punchline: Krusty immediately rejects it without explanation
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Krusty knows exactly what trouble the trampoline causes

jokes.text: Homer: 'That's the last I'll see of Mr. Trampoline.'
jokes.setup: Homer confidently declaring the trampoline problem solved
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizes it as 'Mr. Trampoline' and sets up its inevitable return
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for the running gag - anthropomorphizing makes it funnier

jokes.text: If this were a cartoon, the cliff would break off now.
jokes.setup: Homer standing on cliff edge with trampoline
jokes.punchline: Homer references cartoon physics while in a cartoon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - character acknowledging cartoon logic while being subject to it

jokes.text: All right. All right. You win for now... but someday you'll rust! Rust, I tell ya! [Hysterical laughter]
jokes.setup: Homer has failed multiple times to dispose of trampoline
jokes.punchline: Homer threatens inanimate object with time and corrosion
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Homer's frustration - treating object as nemesis with long-term revenge plan

jokes.text: All right. I got me a bed.
jokes.setup: Bart easily removes trampoline with bike lock trick
jokes.punchline: Bullies immediately steal and repurpose the trampoline
jokes.explanation: Solid continuation of the trampoline saga with character-appropriate outcome

jokes.text: If it were up to you all we'd ever do is work and go to church. That's not true. Name one thing you've done in the past month that was fun.
jokes.setup: Homer complains about Marge being no fun
jokes.punchline: Marge can't immediately think of anything fun she's done
jokes.explanation: Good character setup for Marge's self-doubt about being fun

jokes.text: I made sloppy joes. [Scoffs] That's not fun.
jokes.setup: Marge tries to think of something fun she did
jokes.punchline: Her example is mundane cooking, dismissed by Homer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - finding joy in domestic tasks that others find mundane

jokes.text: [Montage of Marge nagging quotes]
jokes.setup: Kids asked if Marge just nags all the time
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire montage of Marge's actual nagging phrases
jokes.explanation: Great editing joke - overwhelming evidence of Marge's nagging in quick succession

jokes.text: She's one of my favorites. I loved her in the thing I saw her in.
jokes.setup: Brad Goodman introducing Martha Quinn
jokes.punchline: Vague, meaningless celebrity endorsement
jokes.explanation: Good satire of empty celebrity culture and meaningless endorsements

jokes.text: Geriatric Profanity Disorder, or G.P.D.
jokes.setup: Brad listing personality disorders
jokes.punchline: Made-up disorder with official-sounding acronym for old people swearing
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of self-help industry creating disorders for everything

jokes.text: You might remember me from such self-help videos as Smoke Yourself Thin and Get Confident, Stupid.
jokes.setup: Troy McClure's signature introduction format
jokes.punchline: Absurd self-help video titles, especially the contradictory advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure format with perfectly absurd self-help parody titles

jokes.text: Troy: 'Then Brad Goodman came along and gave me this job and a can of fortified wine.'
jokes.setup: Troy describing how Goodman helped his career
jokes.punchline: Payment was cheap alcohol, revealing the low-budget nature of the operation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Troy's desperation and the cheapness of the production

jokes.text: [Loud Gulp] Ahh! Sweet liquor eases the pain.
jokes.setup: Troy mentions getting fortified wine from Brad Goodman
jokes.punchline: Openly celebrates drinking on camera during self-help infomercial
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about alcoholism treated casually in self-improvement context

jokes.text: I'm a Ph.D.... in pain.
jokes.setup: Brad admits he may not have credentials or training
jokes.punchline: Claims doctorate in suffering as qualification
jokes.explanation: Good self-help guru parody with dramatic pause for effect

jokes.text: Troy, this circle is you. My God, it's like you've known me all my life.
jokes.setup: Brad draws a simple circle
jokes.punchline: Troy is amazed by this basic geometric shape
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of meaningless self-help demonstrations and gullible responses

jokes.text: I can see that I'm just a passive-aggressive coculprit. By nagging you when you do foolish things... I just enable your life-script.
jokes.setup: Marge has watched the self-help video
jokes.punchline: She adopts therapeutic jargon to describe her marriage problems
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Marge adopting self-help speak

jokes.text: Surplus drums of mayonnaise from Operation Desert Storm.
jokes.setup: Homer reading another free newspaper item
jokes.punchline: Military surplus condiments from recent war
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing recent military conflict with Homer's cheap acquisitions

jokes.text: After all, we did agree to attend a self-help seminar. What an odd thing to say.
jokes.setup: Homer makes obvious expository statement
jokes.punchline: Lisa calls out the awkward exposition
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor acknowledging clunky TV exposition through Lisa's intelligence

jokes.text: There's no trick to it. It's just a simple trick.
jokes.setup: Brad is explaining his method to the audience
jokes.punchline: He contradicts himself in the same sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-help guru double-talk. The immediate contradiction reveals his nonsensical approach.

jokes.text: Stay the course, Big Ned. You're doing super.
jokes.setup: Brad asks people to listen to their inner voice
jokes.punchline: Ned's inner voice is as annoyingly positive as his outer voice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - Ned's inner voice matches his outer personality completely

jokes.text: [Italian Accent] Hey, Moe. Whatsa matter? You no talka with your accent no more. Mamma mia.
jokes.setup: Moe listening to his inner voice
jokes.punchline: His inner voice is Italian-accented and questions his normal speech
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on inner voice concept - Moe's true self has ethnic accent

jokes.text: We're still going antiquing on Saturday, right, Mother?
jokes.setup: Skinner does rage work against dummy representing his mother
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverts to his usual submissive behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Skinner's inability to maintain independence from his mother

jokes.text: A human going! Bart, be quiet.
jokes.setup: Brad says 'human doing' leads to 'human going'
jokes.punchline: Bart shouts logical progression, Marge reflexively shushes him
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Bart's impulsiveness meets Marge's automatic correction

jokes.text: [Snorts] Earth to boring guy.
jokes.setup: Brad asks if Bart has read his books
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive response in kid slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - casual disrespect delivered with period-appropriate slang

jokes.text: Here we have a man with an obvious eating disorder... and a woman with a bizarre hairstyle-- I'm sure worn only for shock value.
jokes.setup: Brad calls Homer and Marge up to praise their parenting
jokes.punchline: His compliments are actually insults about their appearance
jokes.explanation: Good satire of self-help gurus' backhanded compliments disguised as insights

jokes.text: His name is Bart. His name isn't important.
jokes.setup: Brad keeps calling Bart 'Rudiger'
jokes.punchline: Brad dismisses the correction while being completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Good irony - guru claims names don't matter while getting it wrong

jokes.text: We like Roy! We like Roy!
jokes.setup: Crowd chanting 'Be like boy'
jokes.punchline: Seniors mishear and chant about mysterious 'Roy'
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of mob mentality with age-based hearing joke

jokes.text: Lise, today I am a god. Is that why you're sitting on an ice cream sandwich?
jokes.setup: Bart declares himself godlike
jokes.punchline: Lisa deflates his ego by pointing out mundane reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - Lisa's logical mind puncturing Bart's grandiosity

jokes.text: Take five, Mrs. Feesh. [Ragtime piano playing badly]
jokes.setup: Lovejoy decides to do something he's always wanted instead of his sermon
jokes.punchline: He plays terrible ragtime piano, repeatedly stopping and restarting
jokes.explanation: Lovejoy's suppressed musical ambitions are terrible. The repeated false starts make it funnier. Good character depth.

jokes.text: I want-a change-a my name-a.
jokes.setup: Teacher asks about Marconi's first wireless message
jokes.punchline: Milhouse gives fake Italian-accented answer about name change
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor with Milhouse doing ethnic accent for cheap laugh

jokes.text: You've defined yourself as a rebel. In the absence of a repressive milieu, your societal niche has been co-opted.
jokes.setup: Bart asks why everyone acting like him sucks
jokes.punchline: Lisa explains in overly academic terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lisa moment - sophisticated sociological analysis delivered by 8-year-old

jokes.text: You've fallen through the cracks of our quick-fix, one-hour photo, instant-oatmeal society.
jokes.setup: Lisa continues explaining Bart's identity crisis
jokes.punchline: She lists increasingly mundane examples of society's impatience
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary delivered through Lisa's precocious voice. The escalation from quick-fix to instant oatmeal works.

jokes.text: If elected mayor... my first act will be to kill the whole lot of ya and burn your town to cinders.
jokes.setup: Political candidate at Do What You Feel Festival
jokes.punchline: Sideshow Bob openly threatens genocide as campaign promise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sideshow Bob - turning 'be yourself' philosophy into villainous honesty

jokes.text: I feel like such a free spirit... and I'm really enjoying this so-called 'iced cream.'
jokes.setup: Marge is embracing the free spirit festival
jokes.punchline: She acts like ice cream is an exotic discovery
jokes.explanation: Marge's sheltered nature revealed - she acts like ice cream is revolutionary. The air quotes around 'iced cream' are perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, who am I kidding? The boathouse was the time.
jokes.setup: Character starts to confess love, then changes
jokes.punchline: Implies romantic encounter at boathouse
jokes.explanation: Moderate adult humor about past romantic encounter

jokes.text: Arr! This picture will serve me well on those lonely nights at sea.
jokes.setup: People are being honest about their feelings at the festival
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain admits to inappropriate photography plans
jokes.explanation: Sea Captain's crude honesty fits his character. The maritime context makes it funnier than generic perviness.

jokes.text: Skateboards? You copycat wannabes. Ow! Eat my shorts, young man.
jokes.setup: Everyone is copying Bart's behavior
jokes.punchline: Skinner uses Bart's own catchphrase against him
jokes.explanation: Great role reversal - authority figure using rebel's language against him

jokes.text: By the way, this young lady is not my wife, but I am sleeping with her. I'm telling you this because I'm comfortable with my womanizing.
jokes.setup: Mayor introducing festival
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to adultery as personal growth
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire - politician's honesty about corruption presented as self-improvement

jokes.text: This bandstand wasn't double-bolted. Huh. I didn't feel like it. Hey. I hear you, buddy.
jokes.setup: James Brown falls through unsafe bandstand
jokes.punchline: Worker admits negligence, Homer validates his feelings
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of 'do what you feel' philosophy leading to actual harm

jokes.text: Construction worker: 'This bandstand wasn't double-bolted. Huh. I didn't feel like it.'
jokes.setup: James Brown falls through faulty bandstand
jokes.punchline: Worker admits to skipping safety procedures because he didn't feel like it
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - philosophy applied to safety creates disaster

jokes.text: Yeah. Next you'll be laying a guilt-trip on me for not oiling that Ferris wheel.
jokes.setup: Worker is defensive about the bandstand collapse
jokes.punchline: Mentions another safety failure just as the Ferris wheel starts screaming
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - he mentions the Ferris wheel just as it fails. The escalation from one disaster to another is expertly done.

jokes.text: Here, boy. In here. Huh? Hold on, Son. [Engine Puttering] So long, suckers. Damn. They're very slowly getting away.
jokes.setup: Angry mob chases Bart, he escapes in vehicle
jokes.punchline: The escape vehicle is comically slow, crowd acknowledges this calmly
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy with perfect deadpan commentary on the absurd chase

jokes.text: They're heading for the old mill. No, we're not. Well, let's go to the old mill anyway, get some cider.
jokes.setup: Mob assumes they're going to the old mill
jokes.punchline: Homer corrects them but they decide to go anyway for cider
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mob mentality joke - their bloodlust is easily redirected by the promise of cider. Perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: Self-improvement is better left to people who live in big cities. No. Self-improvement can be achieved, but not with a quick fix.
jokes.setup: Family discussing the lesson of the episode
jokes.punchline: Homer's geographic prejudice vs Lisa's wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Homer's anti-intellectual bias vs Lisa's thoughtfulness

jokes.text: You're off the case, McGonigle. You're off your case, Chief. What does that mean exactly? It means he gets results, you stupid chief!
jokes.setup: Clichéd TV cop show dialogue
jokes.punchline: McGonigle's nonsensical comeback, Homer's aggressive defense
jokes.explanation: Good TV parody with Homer's characteristic over-investment in fictional characters

jokes.text: You're off your case, Chief. What does that mean exactly? It means he gets results, you stupid chief!
jokes.setup: TV cop delivers nonsensical tough-guy line
jokes.punchline: When questioned, makes no sense, Homer defends it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of tough-guy cop dialogue that sounds cool but means nothing. Homer's defensive reaction is perfectly in character.
